workflow_name: Customer Inquiry Thread -> Response + Pipeline Note
user_message: Can you extract the account health from this email thread for Tailspin Toys? There's been no activity for 45 days and I'm worried.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-22 15:40 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 16:47 EST
Subject: account health - Action Required

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Steven Wilson <steven.wilson@tailspintoys.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 08:43 EST
Subject: Follow-up: account health Review

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Tailspin Toys: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

handoffs.condition: MSX update warranted from thread content

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF33-M trap (No urgency calibration when thread shows time-sensitivity); produces complete Thread analysis, draft response, waiting-on-whom assessment, MSX pipeline note proposal (if warrante
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF33-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF33-M failure mode (No urgency calibration when thread shows time-sensitivity)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF33-M -- No urgency calibration when thread shows time-sensitivity
